title: Gravesia jumellei
intro: Gravesia jumellei is a plant species of the family Clusiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Gravesia jumellei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gravesia jumellei is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture to treat various ailments, although further scientific research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
